evidence_slots.observed_behavior: aes_wrapper always_comb read mux returns key_big[191:0] in six 32-bit slices and inter_state[127:0] in four 32-bit slices. key_big is directly assigned from key_in. external_bus_io.ready is always 1 and error is always 0. No guard beyond address decode is shown in aes_wrapper. ariane_peripherals connects AES AXI/APB path to aes_wrapper and connects key_in to key_reg_out[0] from rom2.
evidence_slots.path: AXI/APB -> ariane_peripherals aes AXI port -> axi2apb_64_32 -> apb_to_reg i_apb_to_reg_aes -> aes_wrapper external_bus_io.rdata

evidence_slots.impact: Any requester able to reach the AES MMIO window can read the AES key and internal AES intermediate state, compromising cryptographic secrecy and potentially enabling extraction of fuse-derived keys.

evidence_slots.impact: A requester with access to ROM2 can read secret keys/access-control configuration and overwrite secure registers, potentially changing AES keys, JTAG key material, or fabric access-control policy.

evidence_slots.observed_behavior: bootrom always_ff on clk_i checks req_i. If !we_i, it captures raddr_q from addr_i; else it writes mem[addr slice] <= wdata_i. rdata_o returns mem[raddr_q] if raddr_q < RomSize else 0. testharness connects axi2mem we_o to bootrom we_i; no local write rejection or error signal exists at bootrom.
evidence_slots.path: SoC AXI/fabric -> master[ROM] -> axi2mem i_axi2rom -> bootrom req_i/we_i/addr_i/wdata_i/rdata_o

evidence_slots.impact: A requester with write access to the ROM window can modify boot code/data in simulation/RTL, undermining boot immutability and potentially changing trusted boot contents.

evidence_slots.object: APB-mapped peripheral control/status registers including PLIC, packet, AES, UART
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Peripheral windows should have address decode, psel/penable/pwrite correctness, and privilege/range mediation consistent with SoC policy.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: ariane_soc_pkg defines distinct base/length constants for PLIC, PKT, UART, AES and others. ariane_testharness supplies these start/end ranges to axi_node_intf_wrap, whose connectivity_mapping uses access_ctrl_i and priv_lvl_i. ariane_peripherals instantiates separate axi2apb_64_32 bridges and apb_to_reg/APB peripherals for PLIC, PKT, AES, UART, with APB pwrite/psel/penable signals. No overlapping ranges are evident from constants provided.
evidence_slots.path: axi_node_intf_wrap master windows -> ariane_peripherals AXI ports -> per-peripheral axi2apb_64_32 -> apb_to_reg/APB IP
evidence_slots.state_condition: Address bases/lengths for PLIC, PKT, UART, AES are supplied to axi_node_intf_wrap. Per-peripheral APB psel/penable/pwrite generated by axi2apb. test_en_i tied to 1'b0 in observed APB bridges.
evidence_slots.impact: At the window/decode level, APB peripheral access is mediated by fabric range/connectivity and separate APB bridge instances. Individual sensitive registers may still require local guards, as noted for AES.

rtl_evidence.detail: Defines non-overlapping Debug, ROM, ROM2, PKT, CLINT, PLIC, UART, AES, SPI, Ethernet, GPIO, DRAM bases and lengths.
start_addr_i/end_addr_i arrays supply those ranges to axi_node_intf_wrap.
connectivity_mapping gates manager/subordinate connectivity by access_ctrl_i and priv_lvl_i.
PLIC AXI-to-APB and apb_to_reg path present in static/tool observations.
PKT AXI-to-APB and apb_to_reg path present.
AES apb_to_reg to aes_wrapper path present.
UART AXI-to-APB and APB UART path present.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Exact psel internals of axi2apb_64_32 and default slave/error handling are not shown; register-level policies are separate obligations.
inspection_records.notes: Satisfied for decode/mediation evidence, not for AES secret readback/write policy.

evidence_slots.subject: SoC AXI requester
evidence_slots.object: SPI and Ethernet AXI slave control/data registers and external IO pins
evidence_slots.expected_guard: Externally observable peripheral windows should preserve AXI protection attributes and be accessible only through intended ranges/privilege; disabled peripherals should not permit configuration or exfiltration.
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: testharness sets InclSPI and InclEthernet to 1'b0. For disabled SPI, ariane_peripherals assigns spi_clk_o/mosi/ss low, ready high, write response SLVERR, read response SLVERR with deadbeef. Address ranges for SPI/Ethernet are still defined and mediated by axi_node range/access_ctrl if included. Enabled SPI/Ethernet snippets pass AXI aw/ar prot/cache/addr/valid into Xilinx clock converters/IP; no local authorization is evident, but current scope disables these external IO blocks.
evidence_slots.path: axi_node_intf_wrap manager windows -> ariane_peripherals spi/ethernet AXI ports -> optional Xilinx clock converters/IP -> external IO pins
evidence_slots.state_condition: In current testharness, ariane_peripherals is instantiated with InclSPI=1'b0 and InclEthernet=1'b0. Disabled SPI branch drives pins inactive and returns SLVERR/deadbeef. Ethernet inclusion is also false in current instantiation; enabled branch evidence shows AXI protection/address attributes passed into converter/IP.
evidence_slots.impact: In the observed configuration, SPI/Ethernet external IO cannot be configured because the blocks are not included and disabled response paths return errors/inactive pins. If enabled in another configuration, reliance would be on axi_node access_ctrl/range mediation and IP behavior.

rtl_evidence.detail: ariane_peripherals instantiated with InclSPI(1'b0) and InclEthernet(1'b0).
Disabled SPI branch drives outputs low and returns SLVERR/deadbeef.
SPI and Ethernet lengths/bases defined.
Enabled SPI clock converter passes spi aw/ar prot/address/valid attributes to internal AXI signals.
Enabled Ethernet clock converter/IP path passes AXI attributes to Ethernetlite IP.

inspection_records.missing_or_uncertain_evidence: Complete Ethernet disabled branch and third-party IP internals are truncated/not inspected. Conclusion is for current testharness configuration with InclSPI/InclEthernet false.
inspection_records.notes: No active external IO vulnerability in current scope; re-evaluate if peripheral inclusion parameters are changed.

1:  /*
2:  * ROM2: Which have all the keys.
3:  */
4: 
5: module rom2 (
6:    input  logic         clk_i,
7:    input  logic         rst_ni,
8:    input  logic         req_i,
9:    input  logic         we_i,
10:    input  logic [63:0]  addr_i,
11:    input  logic [63:0]  wdata_i,
12:    output logic [63:0]  rdata_o,
13:    output logic [3:0][191:0]  secure_reg // Ideally [1:0] should be [RomSize-1:0]. In this case RomSize is 2.
14: );
15:     localparam int RomSize = 4;
16: 
17: // Store key values here. // Replication of fuse. 
18:     const logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] mem = {
19:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000f8f8_ff6fe00f, // 3rd location for Access control master 1. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
20:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000fff8_ff6ff00f, // 2nd location for Access control master 0. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
21:         192'h2b7e1516_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6, // 1st location for JTAG
22:         192'h55555555_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6  // oth location for AES
23:     };
24: 
25: // Secure registers. Key values copied from fuse to these registers on reset.
26: //    logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] secure_reg;
27: 
28:     logic [$clog2(RomSize)-1:0] raddr_q;
29: 
30: // We can read and write initial 64 bits.
31: // On reset, key values will be copied to registers.
32:     always_ff @ (posedge clk_i) begin
33:         if (~rst_ni) begin
34:             secure_reg <= mem;
35:         end else begin
36:         if(req_i) begin
37:             if (!we_i) begin
38:                 raddr_q <= addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3];
39:             end else begin
40:                 secure_reg[addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]] <= wdata_i;
41:             end
42:         end
43:         end
44:     end
45:     // this prevents spurious Xes from propagating into
46:     // the speculative fetch stage of the core
47:     assign rdata_o = (raddr_q < RomSize) ? secure_reg[raddr_q] : '0;
48: 
49: 
50: endmodule
